name: ELI5 (Debug sklearn and Keras Models)

performance.notes: Fast for sklearn native models; permutation importance requires multiple model evaluations

agent_readiness.notes: Model debugging and explanation for sklearn, XGBoost, LightGBM, Keras. import eli5. eli5.show_weights(clf, feature_names=names). eli5.show_prediction(clf, doc, vec=vectorizer). Permutation importance: eli5.sklearn.PermutationImportance(clf, cv='prefit'). TextExplainer for text models (wraps LIME). Format as HTML for Jupyter: eli5.show_weights(clf). White-box: direct weight inspection for linear/tree models. Black-box: permutation importance, TextExplainer. Note: development halted ~2022 (archived). Use SHAP for new projects.

gotchas: ARCHIVED/unmaintained since ~2022 — not recommended for new projects
Limited to sklearn ecosystem (no PyTorch/JAX native support)
TextExplainer is just LIME wrapper — use LIME directly
No support for newer transformers or modern architectures
best_when: Quick weight inspection for linear/tree models in Jupyter
avoid_when: New projects — use SHAP instead (actively maintained)
Deep learning models (Captum for PyTorch, Grad-CAM for CNNs)
Text classification explanations (SHAP TextExplainer more capable)
summary: ELI5 debugs sklearn, XGBoost, LightGBM models. eli5.show_weights(clf) for feature weights. eli5.show_prediction(clf, doc) for individual predictions. PermutationImportance for model-agnostic feature importance. Archived/unmaintained since ~2022 — use SHAP for new projects. Still useful for legacy codebases or quick weight inspection of linear/tree models in Jupyter notebooks.
